meta.clinicianNotes: Acute otitis externa (swimmer's ear) — simple, self-contained case good for early practice, including pain-on-touch and recent water exposure clues.

patient.name: Nathan Ross
patient.additionalNotes: Nathan is chill about it, treats it as a minor annoyance getting in the way of his shifts at the pool.

history.reasonForAppointment: Right ear has been sore and blocked for the past 4 days, started after a week of back-to-back lifeguarding shifts at the pool.

hearing.declineDetails: Right ear feels blocked and muffled, started a few days ago alongside the pain. Left ear completely normal. He suspects it's just swelling from the infection.

earHealth.details: Right ear pain, worse when he touches or pulls on his ear, or wears his cap. Some clear-ish discharge the last day or two. No fever. Started after a week of heavy pool exposure and using cotton buds to dry his ears out after shifts.

history.otherConcerns: Nathan wants to know how quickly he can get back in the pool for work and whether he needs antibiotics or ear drops.
